findings.evidence: Grep hits: `Regulator.lean:70` proves `rank_eq_zero_of_finrank_le_one`; `Regulator.lean:72-73` derive `0 < finrank ℚ K` and `finrank ℚ K = 1`; `Regulator.lean:83` has the degree-one criterion.
findings.fix: Keep `rank_eq_zero_of_finrank_eq_one` as the canonical API and have callers derive the equality locally from `Module.finrank_pos` and `omega`, or justify why this parallel monotone name is needed outside local proofs.
findings.issue: `rank_eq_zero_of_finrank_le_one` is just the existing degree-one criterion with `finrank ℚ K ≤ 1`; for a number field `Module.finrank_pos` turns that hypothesis into `finrank ℚ K = 1`.

findings.evidence: Grep hit: `Regulator.lean:79-80` defines `rank_eq_zero_of_finrank_lt_two` by `rank_eq_zero_of_finrank_le_one K (by omega)`; `Regulator.lean:83` has the canonical `finrank_eq_one` form.
findings.fix: Remove this theorem and rewrite uses through the canonical degree-one lemma after converting `< 2` to `= 1` using `Module.finrank_pos` and `omega`.
findings.issue: `rank_eq_zero_of_finrank_lt_two` is only an arithmetic wrapper around `rank_eq_zero_of_finrank_le_one`, so it creates a second parallel name for the same degree-one situation.

summary: The current head removed the earlier inequality wrappers, but the newly added degree variants still introduce parallel rank-zero APIs that differ only by an arithmetic restatement of the same degree-one case.
